jokes.text: This isn't kid's stuff, Mom! We're making NFTs.
jokes.setup: Mom treats their work like cute children's art
jokes.punchline: Amy insists it's serious adult business despite being equally silly
jokes.explanation: Good satire of NFT hype culture and generational tech gaps

jokes.text: Hardly. Though, in this case, yes.
jokes.setup: Mom asks if NFTs are digital art
jokes.punchline: Amy dismisses the idea then immediately confirms it
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Meesh, Mom! No one wants the original!
jokes.setup: Mom asks about owning the original artwork
jokes.punchline: Amy acts exasperated that Mom doesn't understand this backwards logic
jokes.explanation: Captures the absurdity of NFT ownership perfectly through character frustration

jokes.text: Blockchain. Blockchain. Block... Blockchain? That is so interest...
jokes.setup: Mom tries to process the blockchain explanation
jokes.punchline: She gets stuck in a loop then trails off mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Relatable moment of tech overwhelm delivered through verbal stumbling

jokes.text: They look kinda cool, yet also extremely stupid.
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment that captures NFT aesthetic perfectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of NFT aesthetic in six words

jokes.text: Cool yet stupid, you say? Clearly, I, Bender, am destined to be the next smash-hit NFT!
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately identifies himself as perfectly qualified
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - instantly seeing opportunity in insult

jokes.text: You know, he might be just stupid enough. - Might be.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests Bender might be stupid enough
jokes.punchline: Leela's underwhelming agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understatement from Leela

jokes.text: Oops. Wrong gun.
jokes.punchline: Leela fires wrong weapon instead of starting gun
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy, nothing special

jokes.text: That's 'cause I bought it. You know, to trick suckers into thinking it's valuable. Then I paid a quarter.
jokes.setup: Bender's NFT sells for one cent
jokes.punchline: He reveals manipulative scheme then escalates his own spending
jokes.explanation: Great Bender moment - admitting to market manipulation while falling for it himself

jokes.text: This whole NFT thing is completely crooked? I love it!
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiasm for criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - loving something precisely because it's dishonest

jokes.text: You're not gonna believe this. You just made 90... - Million? - Four... - Thousand? - Dollars! - Cents?
jokes.setup: Building up to big earnings reveal
jokes.punchline: Each number gets progressively smaller through interruptions
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect pacing through interruptions

jokes.text: Wait. $94? Woo-hoo! I'm rich!
jokes.punchline: Genuine excitement over tiny amount
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's skewed perspective on money

jokes.text: In your face, kids who helped me!
jokes.punchline: He gloats to children who actually did the work
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dickish Bender moment with economic delivery

jokes.text: You children really earned that ice cream. I probably should have paid for it. Anyway, thanks for the ice cream.
jokes.setup: Acknowledging the kids earned ice cream
jokes.punchline: Casual admission he stole their reward then thanks them for it
jokes.explanation: Escalating awfulness from Bender with perfect casual delivery

jokes.text: That kind.
jokes.setup: Who would buy all of Bender's NFTs
jokes.punchline: Cut to pretentious art museum patrons
jokes.explanation: Simple visual punchline with good economy

jokes.text: Note how the apathy conveyed by the fish is exceeded only by the laziness of the art itself.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally accurate critique presented as praise
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of art world pretension with accidental honesty

jokes.text: Excuse me? No. This piece belongs to the museum. Could you step aside, sir? You're blocking the art.
jokes.punchline: Guard treating him as obstacle to viewing his own image
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the subject being told he's blocking view of himself

jokes.text: That concept now resides in our NFT, so... scoot!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming ownership of his concept
jokes.punchline: Curator casually dismisses him with 'scoot'
jokes.explanation: Absurd legal claim delivered with casual dismissal

jokes.text: Does it look like we're in a stupid TV show? - Kind of.
jokes.setup: Amy rhetorically asking if they're in a stupid TV show
jokes.punchline: Leela's honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with perfect deadpan delivery from Leela

jokes.text: I can't even factor six! I've tried!
jokes.punchline: Bender admits inability to do basic math
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - robot who can't do basic math

jokes.text: Then truly I have nothing! Goodbye, jerks! I'm off to rediscover the concept of Bender!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic exit to find his 'concept' while insulting friends
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender melodrama with casual insults

jokes.text: Mexxxico, with three Xs. - Got it. Adios, carnitas bags.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about his ancestral land
jokes.punchline: Spelling joke plus food-based insult
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay and character-appropriate insult

jokes.text: Hola, amigo. - Close enough.
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive acceptance of friendship
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - accepting friendship with typical lack of enthusiasm

jokes.text: ¡También es magnífico!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic Spanish confirmation of its magnificence
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Bender's catchphrase in Spanish

jokes.text: Because, they were bad at building stuff.
jokes.setup: Everywhere we find ruins of their great civilization
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation for why ruins are everywhere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of ancient civilization trope with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: ¿Quién es este idiota?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to be polite to pottery maker
jokes.punchline: Potter immediately insults him in Spanish
jokes.explanation: Standard fish-out-of-water comedy with language barrier

jokes.text: Aw. It's cute how bad they suck. - Well, it's basically impossible to score. The ball is just a big heavy rock.
jokes.punchline: Revealed that the game is actually impossible
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes Bender's mockery

jokes.text: That's so weird. The house and me have the same last name!
jokes.punchline: Bender's oblivious reaction to obvious family connection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's obliviousness

jokes.text: Parasite!
jokes.punchline: One family member immediately hostile
jokes.explanation: Simple family dysfunction joke with good economy

jokes.text: Nieto. You move away when you just eight seconds old.
jokes.setup: Grandma explaining why they never met
jokes.punchline: Robot babies apparently leave home after eight seconds
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on robot lifecycle

jokes.text: Bah, I don't like talk about feelings. Time for a drink.
jokes.punchline: Abrupt transition to alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Good character trait reveal with abrupt topic change

jokes.text: You're a booze bag? Me, too!
jokes.punchline: Immediate bonding over alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - finding family through shared vice

jokes.text: You can't just steal an NFT off the wall. To steal an abstract mathematical construct, you need a professor.
jokes.punchline: Professor's ego disguised as expertise
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - academic pretension masking self-promotion

jokes.text: I even purchased tiny NFTs at tremendous expense to simulate the museum's collection.
jokes.setup: Professor showing off his heist model
jokes.punchline: He actually bought NFTs for a toy museum
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - buying NFTs for a dollhouse shows complete misunderstanding

jokes.text: And there's a fully functioning tiny cafeteria!
jokes.setup: Professor showing off his detailed model
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily detailed tiny cafeteria
jokes.explanation: Good Professor over-engineering. The specificity of 'fully functioning' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Not in the TV itself, as certain unnamed idiots might think. - Rude.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining where NFT is actually stored
jokes.punchline: Leela's mild reaction to being called an idiot
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Leela's understated offense

jokes.text: I don't care if they can't see me. I paid for this custom one-eyed mask, and I'm wearing it!
jokes.punchline: Leela insists on wearing useless disguise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Leela's stubbornness about her special mask

jokes.text: C-Could you tell me about her? I-I barely remember anything because she was so uninteresting.
jokes.punchline: Casual insult about her being boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - even emotional moments include casual cruelty

jokes.text: Because I steal it.
jokes.setup: Why didn't his mother give him the quipu
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of criminal admission

jokes.text: I steal again. [giggles] - Ooh, you are a bad grandma!
jokes.punchline: She admits to stealing it back, Bender's delighted reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Bender appreciating criminal behavior

jokes.text: Ha! You miss more shots than a stormtrooper!
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference about bad aim
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference, not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's gonna be fundito on a bundito!
jokes.punchline: Made-up Spanish-ish phrase
jokes.explanation: Decent made-up phrase with Spanish flavor

jokes.text: Man, why every time I talk feelings, you gotta change the damn subject? - I'm sorry, URL. I'll do better.
jokes.punchline: Personal relationship drama while missing crime
jokes.explanation: Good character development during inappropriate timing

jokes.text: [flatly] No! - I have accidentally lost the ball!
jokes.punchline: Bender's flat delivery followed by obvious excuse
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between flat delivery and dramatic excuse

jokes.text: Swish, baby! In my face!
jokes.punchline: Celebrating by taunting himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - getting trash talk backwards

jokes.text: Queso on a cross! Bender has won the tournament!
jokes.punchline: Mexican-flavored exclamation
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural exclamation with cheese pun

jokes.text: I'm feeling 2,000 hurts right now.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pain pun
jokes.explanation: Simple pun, well-delivered by Zoidberg

jokes.text: I guess my parents never showed much affection, so in my own sex life... Robbery in progress, baby. Thank God.
jokes.punchline: Grateful interruption by crime
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - URL's relief at avoiding intimacy

jokes.text: How can you say such a thing? I tried almost my very best.
jokes.punchline: Admission of not trying very hard
jokes.explanation: Good understatement revealing the truth

jokes.text: I guess we'll never know what he was trying to say.
jokes.setup: Bender's famous catchphrase cut off by fall
jokes.punchline: Acting like his words were mysterious
jokes.explanation: Good callback joke pretending the famous line is mysterious

jokes.text: But I hit delete! Really hard!
jokes.punchline: Thinking force affects digital deletion
jokes.explanation: Good Fry stupidity - misunderstanding how computers work

jokes.text: I should not have booby-trapped my toothstick.
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual admission of over-engineering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - unnecessary complications

jokes.text: Ass!
jokes.punchline: Cursing while falling
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy callback

jokes.text: Did I say dumb? I-I meant majestic.
jokes.punchline: Desperate backtracking when they move
jokes.explanation: Good fear-based backtracking from Bender

jokes.text: It was 109, but thanks for rounding up.
jokes.punchline: Bender's pedantic correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender pedantry in inappropriate moment

jokes.text: and that one lets you play solitaire on your phone. - And that one? - Es macramé.
jokes.punchline: Last one is just craft project
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from high-tech to mundane crafts

jokes.text: Aw, come on! You've already got a wall full of algorithms!
jokes.setup: Learning the quipu must stay with the emperors
jokes.punchline: Bender's complaint that they're hoarding algorithms
jokes.explanation: Good complaint about algorithm hoarding. Bender's greedy perspective comes through.

jokes.text: But it was given to me by my poor old mother, What's Her Name. [sniffles]
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know her name
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - fake emotion undermined by not knowing mother's name

jokes.text: We no move, estupido.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they can't actually move
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of chase scene tension

jokes.text: Um, actually... - What?! - Why is it still here? - I hate this place!
jokes.setup: Celebrating that museum can't show NFT anymore
jokes.punchline: Revealed it's still displayed, Bender's tantrum
jokes.explanation: Good deflation with typical Bender overreaction

jokes.text: No conceivable reason.
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending - brutally honest assessment of NFT appeal
